papers.title: ILION: Deterministic Pre-Execution Safety Gates for Agentic AI Systems
papers.innovation: Introduces "deterministic pre-execution safety gates" to mitigate safety risks for autonomous AI agents performing real-world actions. This moves beyond traditional content moderation to address the actual execution phase, crucial for preventing unintended or harmful consequences in physical and digital environments.
papers.benchmarks: Not explicitly stated, but the effectiveness would be measured by the reduction in harmful real-world actions, successful containment of agentic failures, and improved reliability of deployed autonomous AI systems.
papers.use_case: Ensuring the safety and preventing dangerous or unintended real-world actions (e.g., filesystem operations, API calls, database modifications, financial transactions) by autonomous AI agents. Essential for the responsible and secure deployment of any agentic AI system.

papers.title: A Dual-Path Generative Framework for Zero-Day Fraud Detection in Banking Systems
papers.innovation: Proposes a dual-path generative framework specifically designed for low-latency, GDPR-explainable zero-day fraud detection in high-frequency banking environments. It effectively tackles challenges like extreme class imbalance and the complete lack of historical data for novel attack patterns.
papers.benchmarks: Not explicitly named, but the framework aims to significantly improve detection rates for 'zero-day attacks' while rigorously adhering to 'low-latency' requirements and providing regulatory-compliant explainability.
papers.use_case: Real-time identification and explanation of novel fraud attempts in financial transactions within banking systems, balancing rapid detection speed with regulatory transparency and compliance mandates (e.g., GDPR).

papers.title: Multi-Axis Trust Modeling for Interpretable Account Hijacking Detection
papers.innovation: Presents a "Hadith-inspired multi-axis trust modeling framework" that uses interpretable, multidimensional criteria to assess the trustworthiness of information sources for account hijacking detection. This approach provides a nuanced evaluation instead of relying on a single, opaque anomaly score.
papers.benchmarks: Not explicitly named; however, performance would be evaluated by improved accuracy in detecting account hijacking and, crucially, the human interpretability and actionability of the generated multi-axis trust scores for security analysts.
papers.use_case: Enhancing cybersecurity by detecting account hijacking with transparent, multi-faceted trust assessments. This framework aids security analysts in investigating incidents, debugging models, and meeting compliance requirements for sensitive user-facing systems.
